title: Accessibility Statement
content:
The University of Studingham is committed to ensuring that all users — including students, tutors, and administrative staff — can access and use the Portal with ease, regardless of disability or individual needs.

We aim to provide an inclusive, barrier-free digital environment that supports accessibility, usability, and equal participation.

Our Accessibility Commitments

1. Keyboard Navigation

The Portal is fully operable using a keyboard alone.

Users can navigate through all pages and interactive elements using:

  • Tab – move forward through elements
  • Shift + Tab – move backward
  • Enter / Space – select or activate items
  • Arrow keys – navigate menus, dropdowns, and other components where applicable

This ensures that users with mobility impairments or those who cannot operate a mouse can still use the platform smoothly.

2. Visual Accessibility & Colour Contrast

We strive to make all visual content accessible to individuals with visual impairments, including colour blindness.

  • All colours used in the Portal meet or exceed WCAG 2.1 AA contrast guidelines.
  • Designs are regularly tested using colour-blindness simulators (such as Sim Daltonism, Coblis, and browser-based tools).
  • The interface avoids relying solely on colour to convey meaning; status indicators also include text labels or icons.

3. Screen Reader & Assistive Technology Compatibility

The Portal is structured to work with major assistive technologies used by individuals who are blind or have low vision, including:

  • Speech output tools (e.g., NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
  • Text-to-speech readers
  • Browser-based accessibility plugins

Technical support includes:

  • Semantic HTML structure
  • ARIA labels where appropriate
  • Proper heading hierarchy
  • Alternative text for meaningful images

This ensures content is understandable and navigable when read aloud.

4. Cognitive Accessibility

The Portal is designed with simple, clear, and consistent layouts to support users with cognitive or learning disabilities.

Key features include:

  • Predictable navigation patterns
  • Easy-to-understand menus
  • Straightforward language throughout the interface
  • Clear labels, instructions, and error messages
  • Avoidance of overly complex interactions

Consistency helps reduce cognitive load and makes tasks easier to complete.

5. Regular Testing & Continuous Improvement

Accessibility is reviewed on an ongoing basis using:

  • Internal audits
  • Industry-standard tools (contrast checkers, simulators, keyboard testing)
  • User feedback

Improvements and updates are implemented regularly to ensure compliance with accessibility best practices and WCAG guidelines.

6. Feedback & Support

We welcome feedback on the accessibility of the University of Studingham Portal.

If you experience difficulty accessing content, have trouble using a feature, or wish to request an alternative format, you may contact the University’s accessibility support team through the official support channels provided within the Portal.

We aim to respond promptly to all accessibility-related concerns.
